name: Cangas de Onís to Arriondas — circular via Collado Zardón
name_original: Cangas de Onís a Arriondas — circular por el Collado Zardón
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cangas de Onís offers diverse terrain at the gateway to the Picos de Europa National Park. The region features rugged peaks, deep valleys, and limestone formations, alongside lush green valleys and dense native forests. The Sella River carves through scenic gorges, providing varied landscapes for cyclists. This area combines challenging mountain ascents with routes through pastures and traditional villages. Best touring cycling routes around Cangas De Onís The most popular touring cycling route is Lagos de Covadonga Trail, a 17.3 miles (27.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This route features a demanding climb, known from La Vuelta a España, with significant elevation gain towards the glacial Lakes of Covadonga. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cangas de Onís to Arriondas — circular via Collado Zardón, a difficult 24.5 miles (39.5 km) path. This route traverses secondary mountain roads, offering views of the surrounding alpine scenery and traditional Asturian villages. Local touring cyclists also love the Sella Bridge – San Antolín de Bedón Beach loop from P2 El Bosque, a 62.6 miles (100.7 km) trail leading through the Sella River valley and towards the coast, often completed in about 6 hours 41 minutes. Touring cycling around Cangas de Onís is defined by dramatic mountain ranges, river gorges, and green valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from challenging climbs to more moderate rides. The routes in Cangas de Onís are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cangas de Onís's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cangas De Onís?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes in the Cangas De Onís area. These include a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Cangas De Onís?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape, from the rugged peaks and deep valleys of the Picos de Europa National Park to lush green valleys, dense native forests, and scenic river gorges carved by the Sella River. You'll encounter challenging mountain ascents, as well as routes through pastures and traditional Asturian villages.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: While many routes in Cangas De Onís are challenging, there are options for beginners and families. The region features routes that follow small, secondary mountain roads, providing scenic views without extreme difficulty. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' or 'moderate' for a more relaxed experience.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Cangas De Onís is renowned for its challenging climbs. The legendary &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029991757/lagos-de-covadonga-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagos de Covadonga Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a regular feature in La Vuelta a España, is a demanding 27.8 km route with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. Other difficult routes ascend through the Picos de Europa, offering significant elevation changes and spectacular alpine views.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer access to stunning natural features. You can cycle near the serene glacial &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/195967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Ercina&lt;/a&gt; within the Picos de Europa, or explore areas around the Sella River and its scenic gorges. The region is also close to the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6051790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cares Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, known for its dramatic landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore while cycling?
A: Absolutely. You can visit the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14988698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt; over the Sella River, a symbol of Asturias. The historically significant site of Covadonga, with its Holy Cave and Neo-Romanesque Basilica, is also nearby and accessible via some routes, offering a blend of nature and spiritual significance.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029988894/cangas-de-onis-to-arriondas-circular-via-collado-zardon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cangas de Onís to Arriondas — circular via Collado Zardón&lt;/a&gt; is a 39.5 km path that traverses secondary mountain roads, offering views of alpine scenery and traditional villages. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31863321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sella Bridge – San Antolín de Bedón Beach loop from P2 El Bosque&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 100.7 km route that takes you through the Sella River valley and towards the coast.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Cangas De Onís?
A: The touring cycling routes in Cangas De Onís are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the dramatic mountain ranges, the beauty of the river gorges, and the lush green valleys that define the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Cangas De Onís?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Cangas De Onís is generally from spring through early autumn (April to October). During these months, the weather is typically more stable, with pleasant temperatures and less rainfall, making for ideal cycling conditions to enjoy the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Is it possible to find routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Many routes in Cangas De Onís are designed to showcase the region's breathtaking scenery. The area is characterized by spectacular alpine views, especially on routes that climb into the Picos de Europa or follow the Sella River through its gorges. You'll frequently encounter panoramic vistas of rugged peaks, lush valleys, and the coastline.

Q: What should I bring for touring cycling in Cangas De Onís?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for challenging climbs, it's advisable to bring appropriate cycling gear, including a helmet, comfortable cycling attire, and layers for changing weather conditions. A repair kit, plenty of water, and snacks are essential. For navigation, a reliable GPS device or the komoot app is highly recommended.

Q: Are there routes that follow rivers or gorges?
A: Yes, the Sella River carves through scenic gorges in the region, and many cycling routes follow its course, offering picturesque views. The area is also known for the spectacular Desfiladero de los Beyos and the famous Cares Gorge, providing opportunities to cycle alongside dramatic river landscapes.
tips: The houses of the Indianos have beautiful buildings with a very distinguishable architecture and beautiful colors. The Indians were emigrants who made their fortune in Latin America (Cuba and Mexico, especially). It is also worth seeing the Church of the Holy Cross, the Roman bridge, the Aula del Reino de Asturias, the Parish Church, the Pintu Palace, the Market Square and the Dago House.

A small town near the Picos de Europa, at the foot of the Sierra del Sueve and a place where the Sella River passes through. There are many companies that organize canoe routes on the river – you can choose between routes of 6, 10 or 14 km or do the complete route of the descent of the Sella.

The Roman bridge of Cangas de Onís, or puentón, is located over the Sella river and is one of the symbols of Asturias. Although it is known as the Roman bridge, it is a medieval construction from the time of the reign of Alfonso XI of Castile with its famous peralted arch and two other minor arches that are unequal. In 1939, a large Victory Cross, symbol of Asturias, was placed in the center of its great central arch.

The church was built in the 13th century and is considered one of the most emblematic monuments of the city. Don Pelayo was an Asturian nobleman who led the resistance against the Muslim invasion of the Iberian Peninsula in the 8th century. He is considered one of Spain's national heroes and is credited with founding the kingdom of Asturias, of which he was its first king. The connection between the Church of the Assumption and Don Pelayo lies in the fact that it is believed that the church was built on the place where the Battle of Covadonga took place, led by Don Pelayo in the year 722, which is considered the first victory of the Christians. on Muslims in the Iberian Peninsula. In this way, the church becomes an important place of pilgrimage for Asturians and for lovers of Spanish history.

waypoints.name: Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción and Monument to Don Pelayo
